query: ok so after last night's deploy the auth middleware is throwing 401s on perfectly valid tokens and i've already grepped jwt_verify.py three times — the stacktrace points at line 84 where we call PyJWT.decode with the wrong audience claim after we rotated the issuer string, can you just patch the verify path so leeway is 30s and aud matches AUTH_AUDIENCE from settings, and add a regression test that replays the token from the failing request log before standup?

i keep meaning to rethink pagination holistically but right now /users is dumping 12k rows into the response and CI is timing out the contract suite — just wire offset+limit query params on the list endpoint in users_controller.py, default limit 50 max 200, update the OpenAPI snippet, and make sure the existing test_list_users fixtures still pass without inventing a cursor scheme

test_cart_checkout flakes on CI every other run but passes locally forever and i'm losing my mind — the race is in the stripe mock not waiting for the webhook handler; please stabilize it by making the fake payment intent emit the succeeded event synchronously in conftest, assert order status goes to paid, and pin the sleep out of the test body

we've talked in circles about PaymentService coupling but the immediate fire is that Stripe gets instantiated inside every method which breaks our unit tests — refactor PaymentService so Stripe client is constructor-injected, keep the public charge/refund signatures identical, and fix the three broken tests in test_payment_service.py that construct it directly

oauth keeps refreshing forever after login and the network tab shows a refresh every ~2s; credentials.py is reissuing because expires_at is compared in local time vs utc — pin expiry as utc-aware, stop the thrash loop in the refresh helper, and drop a unit test that freezes time across the boundary so this doesn't come back tomorrow

not trying to redesign the whole notification pipeline, just fix the bug: email_worker.py is swallowing SMTPAuthenticationError and marking jobs complete, so users never get password-reset mails — re-raise after one retry with exponential backoff, log the message id, and extend test_email_worker to assert failed jobs land in the dead-letter queue

the Dockerfile multi-stage build is fine architecturally but prod images are 1.4GB because node_modules from the builder stage leaks into the runtime layer — slim the final stage to only dist/ and production deps, switch base to node:20-alpine, and confirm docker build --target runtime succeeds without copying the whole monorepo

i know we should eventually move to structured logging but tonight the on-call pager is screaming because order_id is missing from checkout errors — add request-scoped context in middleware so logger.error in checkout_service includes order_id and user_id, update the two places that log bare exceptions, and add a quick test that the extra fields show up on the log record

migrations are a mess philosophically but the concrete break is alembic revision 0042 fails on postgres because we rename column before dropping the old index — rewrite 0042 so drop_index runs first, keep 0043 untouched, and run the upgrade/downgrade smoke against the docker-compose db before i merge

frontend is painting a blank cart after the api shape change and product said ship a hotfix not a redesign — GraphQL cart query still selects lineItems.price but backend renamed to unitPrice; update Cart.tsx and the fragment, handle null unitPrice with a fallback of 0, and fix the MSW mock so storybook cart story renders again

rate limiter idea of using redis is good long-term but right now nginx is returning 502 because the lua module panics on missing X-Forwarded-For — guard the header parse in rate_limit.lua, fall back to remote_addr, reload config via the existing makefile target, and document the one-line change in the ops runbook comment at the top of the file

i've been writing a long design doc in my head about search relevance but product needs the typo tolerance bug fixed by EOD: elasticsearch query in search_service.py doesn't use fuzziness so 'iphon' returns zero hits — set fuzziness AUTO on the multi_match, keep filters identical, and add an integration test with a seeded catalog document

CI green is more important than perfect architecture today — flake8 fails on imports in src/utils/dates.py after the black reformat, isort order is wrong vs our profile, just re-run isort on that file (or manually sort stdlib/thirdparty/local), leave functional code alone, and push so the PR checks stop blocking the release train

the websocket reconnect strategy could use a rethink someday but clients are dropping mid-session because heartbeats are 60s and the load balancer idle timeout is 30s — change the ping interval in ws_server.py to 15s, update the client reconnect backoff max to 10s, and extend test_ws_heartbeat to simulate a 35s idle gap without disconnect

security review wants CSRF on the admin forms eventually; immediate issue is the session cookie missing Secure and SameSite after the flask upgrade — set SESSION_COOKIE_SECURE=True and SameSite=Lax in config.py for non-dev, update the cookie assertion in test_auth_cookies, and don't touch the CSRF middleware yet

we've debated feature flags vs config files for months; for this ticket just fix get_feature_flag so unknown keys return the default instead of KeyError when LaunchDarkly is down — short-circuit in flags.py, log a warning once, and add a unit test with a mocked offline client returning empty

inventory sync is 'eventually consistent' by design but the nightly job double-counts SKUs when the CSV has duplicate rows — dedupe by sku keeping the last row in inventory_importer.py, emit a metric for dropped dupes, and fix the golden-file test under tests/fixtures/inventory that currently expects the broken double count

i'm not asking for a new observability platform — prometheus histogram for /checkout is missing the route label so grafana panels are useless; add the low-cardinality path template label in metrics middleware, avoid raw urls, regenerate the recording rule comment, and verify scrape output includes route="/checkout" in a quick pytest

typescript is yelling after the zod bump and the team chat is a wall of opinions about schema-first vs types-first; ignore the philosophy and fix parseUser in schemas/user.ts — z.infer no longer matches optional email, update the schema and the two call sites in api/users.ts, run tsc --noEmit until clean

k8s rollout strategy is a larger conversation; tonight the liveness probe hits /health before migrations finish and the pod crashloops — delay probe initialDelaySeconds to 45 in the deployment yaml, make /health check db connectivity only after a ready flag file is written by the migrate init container, and confirm kubectl apply dry-run passes
Kafka vs NATS for our event bus — pick one and justify.
Design multi-tenant isolation model for the new SaaS tier.
Compare CQRS vs modular monolith for orders domain.
Choose rollout strategy: blue/green or canary for v3 API.
Tradeoffs: DynamoDB single-table vs Postgres for inventory.
Propose auth architecture: BFF cookies vs SPA tokens.
Should we adopt hexagonal ports for billing, or YAGNI?
Pick edge caching approach for product catalog CDN.
Map bounded contexts before we split the monolith.
Evaluate gRPC vs REST for internal service mesh.
Define SLOs and error budget policy for checkout.
Architecture decision: event sourcing for ledger or not.
Outline zero-downtime migration plan off Mongo.
Compare Terraform vs Pulumi for multi-account AWS.
Design feature-flag kill-switch strategy for mobile.
Pick consistency model for cross-region user profiles.
ADR needed: sync vs async for fraud checks.
Sketch strangler-fig path off the legacy ERP.
Decide cell-based vs regional active-active topology.
Tradeoffs of shared libs vs copy-paste for platform SDKs.
